title: Create, edit and star in videos with two Google Vids updates
gist: Google Vids is adding Gemini Omni (text/image-prompted video generation and chat-based step-by-step editing) and personal digital avatars built from a selfie and voice sample, both gated to paid Google AI Pro/Ultra and Workspace business subscribers, with every AI clip carrying an invisible SynthID watermark.

themes.theme: Gemini Omni video generation and editing

insights.claim: Google is rolling out Gemini Omni into Google Vids, letting users generate and edit high-quality video clips using natural-language text prompts combined with image references such as photos or rough sketches.
insights.speaker: Justin Luk
insights.so_what: Extends Google's generative-video stack into a mainstream productivity tool (Vids/Workspace), broadening the surface area where Gemini-based generation drives engagement and potential upsell.
insights.quote: Omni mixes your inputs to generate a video that matches your vision.
insights.locator: Generate videos from text and images

insights.claim: Gemini Omni supports iterative, chat-based video editing — swapping backgrounds, fixing lighting, or adding effects via plain-language instructions — without requiring the user to restart the video from scratch.
insights.speaker: Justin Luk
insights.so_what: Positions conversational, non-destructive editing as the differentiator over one-shot text-to-video generation tools, a workflow advantage worth tracking against competing video-gen products (e.g., Runway, OpenAI Sora, Adobe).
insights.quote: Because Omni supports step-by-step edits, you can now make changes to your videos without starting from scratch.

insights.claim: Gemini Omni's rollout follows Veo 3.1 becoming available to all Google Vids users in February 2026, showing Google is sequentially layering successive generative-video models into the same Workspace product rather than launching them as standalone apps.
insights.speaker: Justin Luk
insights.so_what: Indicates a cadence of roughly every few months for new generative-video capability drops inside Workspace — useful for forecasting the pace of Google's AI feature releases into its productivity suite.
insights.quote: In February, we made the process simpler by rolling out Veo 3.1 to all users, helping everyone generate videos within Vids.
insights.locator: In the past year, you’ve created millions of videos

insights.claim: Google Vids now offers 'personal avatars': users upload a selfie and a short voice recording to create a digital avatar that can deliver typed, scripted messages in their likeness without any live camera recording.
insights.speaker: Justin Luk
insights.so_what: A consumer-facing synthetic-media identity product from Google, relevant to competitive tracking against HeyGen, Synthesia and other AI-avatar startups now facing a well-distributed Workspace incumbent.
insights.quote: Just upload a selfie and a short voice recording. From there, type what you want to say and your avatar will deliver the message — no recording required.

insights.claim: Access to personal avatars is currently limited to users aged 18 or older in certain regions, and avatars are contractually restricted to the account holder's own likeness, showing Google built age and identity guardrails directly into the feature at launch rather than adding them reactively.
insights.speaker: Justin Luk
insights.so_what: Signals Google's proactive posture on deepfake/identity misuse risk for AI avatar products; the pace at which age/region restrictions loosen is a useful proxy for how comfortable Google (and regulators) become with the product's safety profile.
insights.quote: Personal avatars are linked to a user’s Google Account and are restricted to the account holder’s likeness.
insights.locator: Gemini Omni and personal avatars are available

insights.claim: Gemini Omni and personal avatars are restricted to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers and Google Workspace business customers, tying Google's newest generative-video capabilities directly to paid subscription tiers rather than free-tier access.
insights.speaker: Justin Luk
insights.so_what: Reinforces a consistent pattern across Google's AI product line of gating the most capable generative features behind paid tiers, supporting a subscription-upsell revenue thesis for Alphabet's AI investments.
insights.quote: Gemini Omni and personal avatars are available in Google Vids for Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers and Google Workspace business customers.
insights.locator: Gemini Omni and personal avatars are available

insights.claim: Every AI-generated video clip produced in Google Vids includes an invisible SynthID digital watermark, allowing viewers to verify the content was AI-generated.
insights.speaker: Justin Luk
insights.so_what: Confirms SynthID is being standardized as the default provenance layer across Google's generative media products, relevant to how regulators and platforms may treat AI-content labeling requirements.
insights.quote: every generated clip includes an invisible SynthID digital watermark
